title: Classification of Hope in Textual Data using Transformer-Based ...
preview:
Classification of Hope in Textual Data using
Transformer-Based Models
Chukwuebuka Fortunate Ijezue1, Fredrick Eneye Tania-Amanda Nkoyo1,**and Maaz Amjad1,†
1Department of Computer Science, Texas Tech University, Lubbock, Texas, United States
Abstract
This paper presents a transformer-based approach for classifying hope expressions in text. We developed and
compared three architectures (BERT, GPT-2, and DeBERTa) for both binary classification (“Hope" vs. “Not Hope")
and multiclass categorization (five hope-related categories). Our initial BERT implementation achieved 83.65%
binary and 74.87% multiclass accuracy. In the extended comparison, BERT demonstrated superior performance
(84.49% binary, 72.03% multiclass accuracy) while requiring significantly fewer computational resources (443s
vs. 704s training time) than newer architectures. GPT-2 showed lowest overall accuracy (79.34% binary, 71.29%
multiclass), while DeBERTa achieved moderate results (80.70% binary, 71.56% multiclass) but at
